examples.title: Pseudocode: DMA Controller Transfer Algorithm
examples.description: Demonstrates the step-by-step process of a DMA transfer from initialization to completion
examples.code:
ALGORITHM DMA_Transfer
INPUT: source_address, destination_address, transfer_count, transfer_mode
OUTPUT: transfer_complete_flag, error_status

BEGIN
 // CPU Initialization Phase
 PROCEDURE Initialize_DMAC(src_addr, dest_addr, count, mode):
 SET DMAC.Address_Register = src_addr
 SET DMAC.Count_Register = count
 SET DMAC.Control_Register = mode
 SET DMAC.Status_Register = IDLE
 END PROCEDURE

 // DMA Request Handling
 PROCEDURE Handle_DMA_Request(device_id):
 IF DMAC.Status_Register == IDLE THEN
 SET DMAC.Status_Register = BUS_REQUEST
 SIGNAL Bus_Request TO CPU // BR/HOLD signal
 WAIT FOR Bus_Grant // BG/HLDA signal
 CALL Perform_Transfer
 END IF
 END PROCEDURE

 // Core Transfer Operation
 PROCEDURE Perform_Transfer:
 SET DMAC.Status_Register = TRANSFERRING
 
 WHILE DMAC.Count_Register > 0 DO
 // Check transfer mode
 IF transfer_mode == BURST_MODE THEN
 // Keep bus for entire transfer
 CALL Transfer_One_Word
 DECREMENT DMAC.Count_Register
 INCREMENT DMAC.Address_Register
 
 ELSIF transfer_mode == CYCLE_STEALING_MODE THEN
 // Release bus after each word
 CALL Transfer_One_Word
 DECREMENT DMAC.Count_Register
 INCREMENT DMAC.Address_Register
 RELEASE Bus TO CPU
 REQUEST Bus FROM CPU
 WAIT FOR Bus_Grant
 
 ELSIF transfer_mode == TRANSPARENT_MODE THEN
 // Only transfer when CPU is not using bus
 WHILE CPU_IS_USING_BUS DO
 WAIT
 END WHILE
 CALL Transfer_One_Word
 DECREMENT DMAC.Count_Register
 INCREMENT DMAC.Address_Register
 END IF
 END WHILE
 
 // Transfer Complete
 SET DMAC.Status_Register = TRANSFER_COMPLETE
 SIGNAL Interrupt TO CPU
 RELEASE Bus TO CPU
 END PROCEDURE

 // Single Word Transfer
 PROCEDURE Transfer_One_Word:
 SIGNAL DMA_Acknowledge TO I/O Device // DACK
 PLACE DMAC.Address_Register ON Address_Bus
 
 IF Control_Register.Direction == READ_FROM_IO THEN
 READ data from I/O Device_Data_Register
 WRITE data TO Memory[Address_Register]
 ELSE // WRITE_TO_IO
 READ data FROM Memory[Address_Register]
 WRITE data TO I/O Device_Data_Register
 END IF
 END PROCEDURE

 // Main execution
 CALL Initialize_DMAC(source_address, destination_address, transfer_count, CYCLE_STEALING)
 CALL Handle_DMA_Request(device_id)
END ALGORITHM
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the complete DMA controller operation including initialization, bus request/grant handshake, and the three transfer modes (Burst, Cycle Stealing, Transparent). The algorithm shows how the DMAC manages data transfer independently while keeping the CPU informed through interrupts.

examples.title: Python: DMA Transfer Simulation
examples.description: Simulates DMA controller behavior showing register operations and transfer modes
examples.code:
"""
DMA (Direct Memory Access) Controller Simulation
Demonstrates how DMA transfers work between I/O devices and memory
"""

import time
from enum import Enum
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import List, Optional

class TransferMode(Enum):
 BURST = 1 # Holds bus for entire transfer
 CYCLE_STEALING = 2 # Borrows one cycle at a time
 TRANSPARENT = 3 # Only transfers when CPU is idle

class DMAStatus(Enum):
 IDLE = "IDLE"
 BUS_REQUEST = "BUS_REQUEST"
 TRANSFERRING = "TRANSFERRING"
 TRANSFER_COMPLETE = "TRANSFER_COMPLETE"
 ERROR = "ERROR"

@dataclass
class DMAController:
 """Simulates a DMA Controller with internal registers"""
 address_register: int = 0 # Starting memory address
 count_register: int = 0 # Number of words to transfer
 control_register: int = 0 # Transfer direction, mode, priority
 status: DMAStatus = DMAStatus.IDLE
 memory: List[bytearray] = None # Simulated main memory
 
 def __post_init__(self):
 if self.memory is None:
 self.memory = [bytearray(1024) for _ in range(10)] # 10 pages of memory

class DMATransfer:
 """Simulates a complete DMA transfer operation"""
 
 def __init__(self):
 self.dmac = DMAController()
 self.cpu_bus_held = True # CPU initially has bus control
 self.transfer_log = []
 
 def initialize_transfer(self, src_page: int, src_addr: int, 
 dest_page: int, dest_addr: int, 
 count: int, mode: TransferMode):
 """Step 1: CPU programs the DMAC registers"""
 print(f"\n=== DMA Initialization ===")
 print(f"Setting up transfer: {count} bytes")
 print(f"Source: Page {src_page}, Address {src_addr}")
 print(f"Destination: Page {dest_page}, Address {dest_addr}")
 print(f"Mode: {mode.name}")
 
 # Program the DMAC registers
 self.dmac.address_register = (dest_page * 1024) + dest_addr
 self.dmac.count_register = count
 self.dmac.control_register = mode.value
 self.dmac.status = DMAStatus.IDLE
 
 self.transfer_log.append(f"Initialized: {count} bytes, mode={mode.name}")
 
 def request_bus(self) -> bool:
 """Step 2: DMAC requests bus control from CPU"""
 print(f"\n--- DMAC requesting bus (DREQ) ---")
 self.dmac.status = DMAStatus.BUS_REQUEST
 
 # Simulate CPU acknowledging
 time.sleep(0.1)
 self.cpu_bus_held = False
 print(f"CPU granted bus to DMAC (BG/HLDA)")
 self.transfer_log.append("Bus granted to DMAC")
 return True
 
 def perform_transfer(self, source_data: List[int], mode: TransferMode):
 """Step 3-4: DMAC performs the actual data transfer"""
 print(f"\n=== Starting Data Transfer ===")
 self.dmac.status = DMAStatus.TRANSFERRING
 
 bytes_transferred = 0
 word_size = 4 # 4 bytes per word
 
 while self.dmac.count_register > 0:
 # Read data from "I/O device" (simulated as source_data)
 if bytes_transferred < len(source_data):
 data = source_data[bytes_transferred:bytes_transferred + word_size]
 else:
 data = [0] * word_size
 
 # Write to memory
 mem_addr = self.dmac.address_register + bytes_transferred
 self._write_to_memory(mem_addr, data)
 
 print(f"Transfer word {bytes_transferred//word_size + 1}: "
 f"Data {data} -> Memory[{mem_addr}]")
 
 # Update registers
 bytes_transferred += word_size
 self.dmac.count_register -= word_size
 
 # Handle different modes
 if mode == TransferMode.CYCLE_STEALING:
 print(f" [Cycle Stealing: Releasing bus to CPU temporarily]")
 self.cpu_bus_held = True # Simulate releasing bus
 time.sleep(0.05)
 self.cpu_bus_held = False
 
 elif mode == TransferMode.TRANSPARENT:
 print(f" [Transparent: Waiting for CPU to not need bus]")
 time.sleep(0.1)
 
 self.transfer_log.append(f"Transferred word at offset {bytes_transferred}")
 
 print(f"\nTransfer complete! {bytes_transferred} bytes moved.")
 
 def _write_to_memory(self, address: int, data: List[int]):
 """Write data to simulated memory"""
 page = address // 1024
 offset = address % 1024
 
 if page < len(self.dmac.memory):
 for i, byte in enumerate(data):
 if offset + i < 1024:
 self.dmac.memory[page][offset + i] = byte
 
 def transfer_complete(self):
 """Step 5: DMAC signals completion and releases bus"""
 print(f"\n=== Transfer Complete ===")
 self.dmac.status = DMAStatus.TRANSFER_COMPLETE
 self.cpu_bus_held = True # CPU gets bus back
 print(f"DMAC sent interrupt to CPU")
 print(f"CPU resumed control of the bus")
 self.transfer_log.append("Transfer complete, interrupt sent to CPU")
 
 def run_simulation(self, source_data: List[int], mode: TransferMode):
 """Run complete DMA transfer simulation"""
 # Initialize
 self.initialize_transfer(0, 100, 1, 200, len(source_data), mode)
 
 # Request bus
 self.request_bus()
 
 # Perform transfer
 self.perform_transfer(source_data, mode)
 
 # Complete
 self.transfer_complete()
 
 return self.transfer_log


# Demo usage
if __name__ == "__main__":
 # Simulate data from an I/O device (e.g., disk read)
 device_data = [0x48, 0x65, 0x6C, 0x6C, 0x6F, 0x20, 0x57, 0x6F, 0x72, 0x6C, 0x64, 0x00]
 # "Hello World" in ASCII
 
 print("=" * 60)
 print("DMA CONTROLLER SIMULATION - Cycle Stealing Mode")
 print("=" * 60)
 
 dma = DMATransfer()
 dma.run_simulation(device_data, TransferMode.CYCLE_STEALING)
 
 print("\n" + "=" * 60)
 print("Transfer Log:")
 for entry in dma.transfer_log:
 print(f" - {entry}")
examples.explanation: This Python simulation demonstrates the complete DMA transfer process including initialization of DMAC registers (Address, Count, Control), bus request/grant handshake, data transfer in Cycle Stealing mode, and interrupt signaling on completion. The simulation shows how the DMAC manages transfers while the CPU is only involved at setup and completion.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is the number of words transferred

examples.title: C: DMA Controller Registers and Transfer Implementation
examples.description: Demonstrates DMA controller register structures and transfer operations in C
examples.explanation: This C implementation demonstrates DMA controller register structures similar to the Intel 8237 DMA controller. It shows how to program the Address Register (starting memory location), Count Register (number of words), and Control Register (direction and mode). The code demonstrates both Cycle Stealing and Burst mode transfers, illustrating how the DMAC acquires and releases the system bus for data movement.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of words transferred
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) for controller structures, O(m) for simulated memory
